target.title: fix(models): strip plaintext api keys from models json
target.body:
Part of #11829.
Replaces #83294; the contributor branch cannot be updated because maintainer edits are disabled. This keeps the contributor commits and adds maintainer fixups.

Summary:
- Strip newly generated plaintext provider `apiKey` values from `models.json`.
- Preserve existing plaintext keys during merge mode so current local installs do not lose auth unexpectedly.
- Preserve SecretRef/env markers and write a non-secret OpenClaw-managed marker for config-owned plaintext keys so PI still accepts custom provider model rows.
- Add regression coverage for PI registry parsing of the generated `models.json`.

Real behavior proof:
Behavior addressed: generated `models.json` no longer persists newly resolved plaintext API keys, while custom provider rows remain loadable by PI.
Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox on Linux.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `corepack pnpm test src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts src/agents/model-auth-markers.test.ts src/agents/pi-model-discovery.test.ts && corepack pnpm check:changed`
Evidence after fix: Testbox `tbx_01kskw2wgxe8999xxqamy1m5c2`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26491356820, exit 0.
Observed result after fix: focused tests passed, `check:changed` passed, and the regression test proved the generated `models.json` parses through the real PI registry and resolves the custom model.
What was not tested: live provider network calls with a real external custom model endpoint.
